normalization_policy: Stage 5 outputs were not modified. Compact ticket records were expanded into Stage 6 execution contracts with required skill, agent, file scope, validation, visual, responsive, artifact, and blocker fields.

expanded_tickets.title: Implement supplier and source APIs
expanded_tickets.description: Implement workspace-scoped supplier CRUD, supplier source records, source health metadata, audit events, and API validation for the MVP file/manual intake model.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Build API and service boundaries with workspace isolation and audit coverage before import lifecycle work begins.

acceptance_criteria: Supplier CRUD and source records work.
Workspace scoping is enforced for every supplier and source operation.
Audit events are emitted for supplier and source mutations.
Source records support MVP file/manual intake metadata without depending on named portal connectors.
validation_steps: Run audit event tests for supplier mutations.

expanded_tickets.title: Implement import batch lifecycle and asset commit
expanded_tickets.description: Implement upload/manual import batch creation, mapping, validation, row errors, commit preview, asset commit, retry-safe async state handling, and raw import preservation.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Protect normalized assets from silent overwrite and make failed imports recoverable.

acceptance_criteria: Upload, mapping, validation, row errors, and commit are supported.
Import staging never silently overwrites normalized assets.
Failed imports expose recoverable error states.
Committed assets preserve supplier provenance and import batch traceability.
validation_steps: Run async failure and retry tests.

expanded_tickets.title: Build import review wizard UI
expanded_tickets.description: Build the import review wizard for upload/manual entry, field mapping, validation table, commit summary, error report access, keyboard mapping, and visible async states.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Preserve UI-BLUEPRINT-002 and VISUAL-SPEC-002. Do not rely on color alone for row errors.

visual_requirements.color_direction: Neutral table-heavy UI with blue focus states and semantic validation colors
visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Mobile uses stacked cards for rows and keeps step actions sticky.
visual_do_rules: Make row errors easy to scan
visual_dont_rules: Do not rely on color alone for errors
Do not bury download error report action
visual_acceptance_criteria: Keyboard users can complete all wizard steps
Validation errors remain visible while editing mapping
Commit action is disabled with explanation until prerequisites pass

responsive_requirements.desktop_layout: Step wizard with table-focused validation workspace
responsive_requirements.tablet_layout: Wizard steps remain visible with table priority columns
responsive_requirements.mobile_layout: Stacked row cards with sticky step actions
collapse_rules: Validation table becomes priority-card rows on mobile
Commit summary remains immediately before commit action
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Return paths to supplier detail, asset library, and status center remain available.

acceptance_criteria: Wizard supports upload, mapping, validation, commit, and error report.
Keyboard users can complete mapping.
Visual acceptance criteria for VISUAL-SPEC-002 pass.
Commit action is disabled with an explanation until prerequisites pass.
validation_steps: Record visual QA, responsive validation, design system compliance, and visual drift status.

expanded_tickets.title: Build asset library UI
expanded_tickets.description: Build searchable, filterable, table-first asset library views with detail drawers, asset state labels, supplier provenance, missing-cost visibility, archive warnings, and add-to-bundle/add-to-quote actions.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Preserve UI-BLUEPRINT-003 and keep supplier provenance and cost readiness visible in rows.

visual_requirements.color_direction: Neutral with teal action and status badges
visual_requirements.primary_visual_focus: Fast scan across asset type, supplier, cost readiness, and usage
visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Split view collapses into list then detail route on mobile.
visual_do_rules: Show cost readiness in every row
visual_dont_rules: Do not use card grid for dense asset management
visual_acceptance_criteria: Rows remain readable at tablet width
Filters do not push table below first viewport on desktop
Archived and missing-cost states are text-labeled

responsive_requirements.desktop_layout: Split view with persistent filters, asset table, and detail drawer
responsive_requirements.tablet_layout: Table remains primary with drawer overlay
responsive_requirements.mobile_layout: List route followed by detail route
collapse_rules: Split view collapses to list then detail route
Table becomes priority-column list on mobile
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Asset list, detail, bundle, and quote actions remain discoverable.

acceptance_criteria: Users can search, filter, inspect, tag, and archive assets.
Missing cost and archived states are visible.
Supplier provenance remains visible in list and detail contexts.
validation_steps: Capture preview and visual QA evidence.

expanded_tickets.title: Implement pricing, cost, margin, and snapshots
expanded_tickets.description: Implement cost components, pricing rules, margin policies, low-margin detection, approval status, immutable pricing snapshots, and audit coverage for commercial workflows.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Pricing math and immutable snapshots are high-trust paths; add deterministic fixtures and audit checks.

acceptance_criteria: Cost components, pricing rules, margin policies, and immutable price snapshots exist.
Low-margin conditions are detected.
Pricing snapshots are immutable once used by generated outputs.
Calculation paths include deterministic test fixtures.
validation_steps: Run workspace authorization tests for pricing visibility.

expanded_tickets.title: Build pricing workspace and bundle recipe UI
expanded_tickets.description: Build pricing and bundle/recipe workspaces covering cost breakdowns, pricing controls, margin preview, approval status, audit timeline, component picker, recipe composition, scaling, and publish checklist.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Preserve UI-BLUEPRINT-004 and UI-BLUEPRINT-005. Do not use drag-and-drop as the only builder interaction.

expanded_tickets.frontend_task_hint: Build pricing workspace and bundle recipe UI

visual_requirements.color_direction: Neutral with semantic margin states and teal build actions
visual_requirements.component_style: Outlined and compact soft-card composition where appropriate
visual_requirements.primary_visual_focus: Margin confidence, pricing impact, component composition, and publish readiness
visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Cost, margin, component, pricing, and publish sections stack on mobile with sticky primary actions.
visual_do_rules: Show price, cost, and margin in close proximity
Keep live pricing visible while editing components
visual_dont_rules: Do not make margin status decorative
Do not use drag-and-drop as the only interaction
Do not hide cost impact behind a modal
visual_acceptance_criteria: A user can identify why a price is blocked within 5 seconds
Margin status is available to screen readers
Approval actions are visually distinct from save actions
Components can be added without pointer-only interactions
Cost preview updates are announced or clearly refreshed
Publish checklist shows all blockers

responsive_requirements.desktop_layout: Split view for pricing and recipe builder workspaces
responsive_requirements.tablet_layout: Primary panels stack with live margin visible
responsive_requirements.mobile_layout: Step sections for details, components, pricing, and publish
collapse_rules: Cost and margin panels stack on mobile
Primary approve/publish actions remain sticky or immediately available
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Pricing and offering routes preserve return path to assets and quotes.

acceptance_criteria: Users can preview margin and publish valid bundles/recipes.
Low-margin and invalid-component states are visible and accessible.
Component picker supports non-pointer interaction.
Publish checklist shows all blockers.
validation_steps: Run keyboard checks for component picker.
Run accessibility checks for margin and approval statuses.
Capture preview and visual QA evidence.

expanded_tickets.title: Implement customer quote and generated output backend
expanded_tickets.description: Implement customers, quotes, quote lines, approval states, price snapshot usage, generated output jobs, expiring signed share links, revocation, audit events, and security checks.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Quote sharing is security-sensitive. Validate token scoping, expiration, revocation, and audit events.

acceptance_criteria: Quotes use customers, quote lines, approval states, price snapshots, generated output jobs, and signed share links.
Share links can expire and be revoked.
Generated output jobs preserve status and retry visibility.
Quote sharing emits audit events and avoids exposing workspace-private data outside token scope.
validation_steps: Run audit event tests for approve/share/revoke transitions.

expanded_tickets.title: Build quote builder UI
expanded_tickets.description: Build quote list/detail/customer workflows, line item builder, readiness blockers, margin summary, approval controls, generated output progress, and secure share controls.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Preserve UI-BLUEPRINT-006 and make generated-output sharing states explicit.

visual_requirements.color_direction: Neutral workspace with teal primary actions and margin status accents
visual_requirements.primary_visual_focus: Quote line accuracy, margin state, and generated-output readiness
visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Mobile separates quote details, lines, margin, and sharing into tabs.
visual_do_rules: Show margin summary near line items
Make generation and sharing security states explicit
visual_dont_rules: Do not allow generate/share controls to appear ready when prerequisites fail
Do not hide expired link state
visual_acceptance_criteria: A user can identify quote readiness and blockers at all times
Generated output progress persists outside the current page
Share link expiration and revocation are visible

responsive_requirements.desktop_layout: Split view with quote header, customer panel, line items, margin summary, generated output panel, and share controls
responsive_requirements.tablet_layout: Line items remain primary with stacked supporting panels
responsive_requirements.mobile_layout: Tabs for quote details, lines, margin, and sharing
collapse_rules: Quote details, lines, margin, and sharing separate into mobile tabs
Generate/share controls remain gated by readiness blockers
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Quote list, quote detail, customer detail, and generated output routes remain available.

acceptance_criteria: Quote builder shows readiness, blockers, margin summary, generation progress, and sharing controls.
Keyboard users can edit quote lines.
Generate and share controls are visibly unavailable when prerequisites fail.
Share link expiration and revocation states are visible.
validation_steps: Record responsive validation and visual drift status.

expanded_tickets.title: Build status center and async recovery
expanded_tickets.description: Build a data-dense status center for active and failed jobs, filters, job details, retry/cancel actions, support reference IDs, and secret-safe error display.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Preserve UI-BLUEPRINT-007. Failed jobs must not be hidden behind filters and job logs must not expose secrets.

expanded_tickets.frontend_task_hint: Build status center and async recovery

visual_requirements.color_direction: Neutral operational with semantic job states
visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Mobile shows failed jobs first, then active jobs.
visual_do_rules: Show job status with text labels
visual_dont_rules: Do not use transient-only failure messages
visual_acceptance_criteria: Failed jobs are visible without filtering
Retry eligibility is clear
Job detail drawer is keyboard accessible

responsive_requirements.desktop_layout: Table-first status center with filters, active jobs, failed jobs, detail drawer, and retry actions
responsive_requirements.mobile_layout: Failed jobs first, then active jobs with detail drawer or route
collapse_rules: Failed jobs appear above secondary active job lists on mobile
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Status route remains accessible from dashboard and async banners.

acceptance_criteria: Users can inspect failed jobs and retry eligible jobs.
Job details never expose secrets.
Failed jobs are visible without filtering.
Job detail drawer is keyboard accessible.
validation_steps: Run accessibility checks for job drawer.
Run secret exposure review for job detail content.
Capture preview and visual QA evidence.

expanded_tickets.title: Implement launch-critical automated validation
expanded_tickets.description: Create and run launch-critical validation covering supplier setup, import review/commit, asset library, pricing/margins, bundle/recipe publish, quote generation/sharing, async recovery, accessibility, visual QA, and security checks.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Validation must map results to ticket acceptance criteria, visual requirements, security checks, and release gates.

expanded_tickets.frontend_task_hint: Validate launch-critical flows and frontend evidence

visual_requirements.responsive_behavior: Validate desktop, tablet, and mobile behavior from Stage 4.
visual_do_rules: Map evidence to each visual spec
visual_dont_rules: Do not mark visual QA passed without evidence
Do not ignore missing preview URLs
visual_acceptance_criteria: Every launch-critical frontend ticket has pass/fail visual evidence
Responsive behavior is recorded for required viewports
Design system compliance is recorded

collapse_rules: Validate all Stage 4 collapse rules referenced by launch-critical tickets
responsive_requirements.navigation_behavior: Validate route and workflow navigation for launch-critical flows.

acceptance_criteria: Launch-critical E2E, accessibility, security, and regression checks are automated or documented with required artifacts.
Validation maps back to ticket acceptance criteria and release gates.
Visual QA and responsive evidence exists for launch-critical frontend tickets.
validation_steps: Validate expected artifacts are present or blocked with reason.

expanded_tickets.title: Prepare MVP release candidate checklist
expanded_tickets.description: Prepare release candidate checklist covering release gates, rollback, support notes, known deferred work, artifact registry verification, operational readiness, and Stage 7 handoff needs.

agent_skill_instructions.usage_notes: Do not mark release candidate ready until all release gates and artifact evidence are resolved.

acceptance_criteria: Release checklist includes gates, rollback, support notes, and known deferred work.
All release gates are complete, blocked with reason, or explicitly accepted as risk.
Stage 7 handoff includes operational, deployment, monitoring, analytics, and support needs.
validation_steps: Confirm all release gates are complete.
Confirm unresolved risks are recorded in the risk acceptance ledger if accepted.

remaining_execution_blockers.description: The repository currently contains planning artifacts and README.md only. No application framework, package manifest, runtime, test runner, database client, or dev server exists for implementation tickets.
remaining_execution_blockers.next_action: Select or scaffold the implementation stack before executing TICKET-001 and TICKET-003.
